creator: bobf@mrp3.com
text:
Using the 'workaround' settings I have occasionally seen problems in video playback.  The problems are MUCH less frequent but have not completely been resolved.

In one case a video suddenly went 'out of sync' between sound and video, and shortly afterwards a different video suddenly stopped playback several secones before the end.  I have not seen this happen before nor since.

I have also seen one case of bad audio playback distortion, coupled with a stall in video playback, specifically the kind of audio distortion that sounds as if 0.1 seconds of audio is being repeated for over 1 second... like a "raspberry" sound.  (this was common without the workaround).

So if the problem is related to timing events in video playback, then a problem that occasionally causes severe timing errors is still there.

Using sysutils/nbosd I occasionally check CPU frequency and most of the time the freq seems to be staying at 1550Mhz since the system is mostly idle.
